description: Living or growing in natural conditions; not domesticated or cultivated. 'Waeshi' names the quality of wildness—the untamed, the free-growing, nature uncontrolled.
sound_symbolism: The word runs free. 'Wa' opens with expansive energy, freedom present. The vowel 'e' moves without constraint. Then 'shi' /ʃi/ releases into open space, wildness embodied.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'waeshi pelowa' (wild meadow). Opposite of 'woenu' (tame). Related to 'ralu' (free) but emphasizes natural state rather than liberation from constraint.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Wild spaces sustain biodiversity—untamed nature provides ecosystem services that cultivation cannot.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Wild foods supplemented cultivation—foraging from wild places complemented farming.
